events.name: Insight
events.description:
Here and Now: Training Attention
A 25-minute in-person practice, based on the Healthy Minds program and developed by the Tecmilenio Institute of Purpose and Integral Wellbeing, that invites you to pause the daily rhythm and train mindfulness through guided breathing exercises and awareness of the present moment.
events.location: TEC | (ESPACIO DE REFLEXION)

speakers.name: Rosalinda Ballesteros
speakers.position: Directora del Instituto del Propósito y Bienestar Integral
speakers.bio:
Actualmente es directora del Instituto de Propósito y Bienestar Integral de Tecmilenio, donde fungió como Vicerrectora de Preparatoria hasta 2017. Es doctora en Estudios Humanísticos por el Tecnológico de Monterrey, (2011), graduada de la Maestría en Psicología Positiva Aplicada de la Universidad de Pennsylvania, EUA. Con el proyecto de investigación: Significado y propósito de vida como elementos clave para éxito personal en (2015). Cuenta además con una maestría en Ciencias con Especialidad en Comunicación por el Tecnológico de Monterrey (2000). Está certificada en “Appreciative Inquiry in Positive Business and Society Change” de la Universidad Case Western Reserve (2016), metodología de Desarrollo organizacional clave para las organizaciones positivas. Certificada en Instrucción de la Compasión Integral por parte de “Life Unversity”, (2020) metodología seleccionada por UNESCO como central para acercar a las metas del Desarrollo de Naciones Unidas. Cuenta con 25 años de experiencia docente y amplia experiencia en liderazgo de equipos.

Miembro del claustro de la Maestría en Liderazgo Positivo de la Universidad Tecmilenio, ha sido catedrática en la Maestría en Ética para Construcción Social de la Universidad de Deusto en España y del Doctorado en Estudios Humanísticos del Tecnológico de Monterrey.

Es vicepresidenta de IPEN (International Positive Education Network), miembro del consejo de asesores de IPPA (International Positive Psychology Association) y ha sido


presidenta de la división de educación, además es miembro del consejo y de la mesa directiva de la Sociedad Mexicana de Psicología Positiva y miembro del consejo de World Happiness Foundation, que busca llevar el bienestar a un billón de personas.

Actualmente, escribe columnas mensuales para diversas revistas de negocios como Forbes México, Líderes Mexicanos, Milenio y otras revistas como Vanidades.

Recibió el Premio Mujer Tec 2024 en la categoría de Salud y Bienestar. Sus aportaciones a la ciencia y a la práctica del bienestar con perspectiva de género nos han llevado a la construcción de que Tecmilenio sea una Universidad Positiva.

Ultimas publicaciones académicas:

Toledo, M., Charles-Leija, H., Castro, C. G., Guerrero, I., & Ballesteros-Valdés, R. (2022). The IWH-BEAT Questionnaire Validation. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 19(6), 3559.

Charles-Leija, H., Ballesteros-Valdés, R., Toledo, M., & Guerrero, I. (2022). Comparison of the Socio-emotional Well-being of High School Students at Universidad Tecmilenio before and during the Covid-19 Contingency. Revista Internacional de Educación Emocional y Bienestar Vol, 2(1), 33-51.

Ballesteros-Valdés, R., & Charles-Leija, H. (2021). Tecmilenio case. Wellbeing and Resilience Education: COVID-19 and Its Impact on Education. White, M. A., & McCallum, F. (Eds.). Routledge.

//

She currently serves as Director of the Institute for Purpose and Integral Well-Being at Tecmilenio, where she previously held the position of Vice Chancellor of High School Education until 2017. She holds a PhD in Humanistic Studies from Tecnológico de Monterrey (2011) and a Master’s degree in Applied Positive Psychology from the University of Pennsylvania, USA, where she conducted the research project Meaning and Purpose in Life as Key Elements for Personal Success (2015). She also holds a Master of Science degree with a specialization in Communication from Tecnológico de Monterrey (2000).

She is certified in Appreciative Inquiry in Positive Business and Society Change by Case Western Reserve University (2016), a key organizational development methodology for positive organizations. She is also certified in Integral Compassion Instruction by Life University (2020), a methodology selected by UNESCO as central to advancing the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. She has 25 years of teaching experience and extensive expertise in team leadership.

A member of the faculty of the Master’s Degree in Positive Leadership at Tecmilenio University, she has also served as a professor in the Master’s Program in Ethics for Social Development at the University of Deusto in Spain, and in the PhD Program in Humanistic Studies at Tecnológico de Monterrey.

She is Vice President of IPEN (International Positive Education Network), a member of the Advisory Board of IPPA (International Positive Psychology Association), and has served as President of the Education Division. She is also a member of the Board and Executive Committee of the Mexican Society of Positive Psychology, as well as a board member of the World Happiness Foundation, whose mission is to bring well-being to one billion people.

She currently writes monthly columns for various business publications such as Forbes México, Líderes Mexicanos, Milenio, and other magazines including Vanidades.

She received the Mujer Tec Award 2024 in the category of Health and Well-Being. Her contributions to the science and practice of well-being from a gender perspective have been instrumental in positioning Tecmilenio as a Positive University.

Recent academic publications:

Toledo, M., Charles-Leija, H., Castro, C. G., Guerrero, I., & Ballesteros-Valdés, R. (2022). The IWH-BEAT Questionnaire Validation. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 19(6), 3559.

Charles-Leija, H., Ballesteros-Valdés, R., Toledo, M., & Guerrero, I. (2022). Comparison of the Socio-emotional Well-being of High School Students at Universidad Tecmilenio before and during the COVID-19 Contingency. International Journal of Emotional Education and Well-Being, 2(1), 33–51.

Ballesteros-Valdés, R., & Charles-Leija, H. (2021). Tecmilenio Case. In White, M. A., & McCallum, F. (Eds.), Wellbeing and Resilience Education: COVID-19 and Its Impact on Education. Routledge.

events.name: The Innovation Bridge: How Mexico, the U.S. and LATAM Can Build the Next Healthtech Ecosystem
events.description:
How do we transform scientific talent into real solutions for millions of people?
Latin America and the United States share major health challenges—from chronic diseases to aging populations—yet innovation ecosystems still operate in isolation. This conversation brings together leaders from the Inter-American Development Bank and Tecnológico de Monterrey to explore how cross-border collaboration can unlock a new generation of science-based startups.
The panel will discuss how universities, investors, governments, and entrepreneurs can build a binational innovation pipeline that accelerates deep-tech ventures, validates them in real healthcare systems, and scales solutions across the Americas.

speakers.name: Josue Delgado
speakers.bio:
Josué Delgado es un líder en innovación y emprendimiento en México, impulsando desde incMty iniciativas que conectan emprendedores, startups y ecosistemas para transformar ideas en proyectos de alto impacto. Se desempeña como Corporate Innovation Director en incMty, donde enfoca sus esfuerzos en innovación abierta, desarrollo corporativo y colaboración entre corporativos, academia y scaleups. Egresado del Tecnológico de Monterrey, es reconocido por su experiencia en venture capital y activación de ecosistemas de innovación

//

Josué Delgado is a Mexican innovation and entrepreneurship leader, driving initiatives at incMty that connect entrepreneurs, startups, and ecosystems to turn ideas into high‑impact projects. He serves as Corporate Innovation Director at incMty, focusing on open innovation, corporate development, and collaboration between corporations, academia, and scaleups. A graduate from Tecnológico de Monterrey, he is recognized for his expertise in venture capital and innovation ecosystem activation

speakers.name: Ulrick Noel
speakers.position: Director Ejecutivo del Instituto de Emprendimiento Eugenio Garza Lagüera
speakers.company: Instituto de Emprendimiento Eugenio Garza Lagüera
speakers.bio:
Ulrick, es Director General del Instituto de Emprendimiento del Tec de Monterey, la universidad privada más prestigiosa de América Latina y ocupa 5to. lugar global en emprendimiento. Previamente fue Líder de las practicas de Transformación Digital e Innovación para Deloitte Consulting, prestando servicios a Ejecutivos de C-Level en toda América Latina. Es Socio Director de Everdeen Capital, un banco de inversión boutique y fondo de inversión estratégico; Ulrick también es un experimentado Endeavor Mentor, un profesor senior dedicado en varias escuelas de posgrado y sirve como miembro de la junta en varias empresas y ONGs. Además, es cofundador y expresidente de la Asociación de Emprendedores de México.

Anteriormente dirigió como CEO y cofundador de Rocket Internet en América Latina, lanzando Linio.com y Easy Taxi. Ulrick también dirigió Venture Institute, una aceleradora de startups de primer nivel, como Socio Director y se desempeñó como Gerente General de Latin American Bridge by Coca-Cola. Ha invertido en diferentes fondos de capital riesgo y como Angel Investor. También ha puesto en marcha más de 14 empresas en diferentes industrias. Ulrick se desempeñó como gerente de Bain & Company y A.T. Kearney, firmas líderes en consultoría de estrategia; y CEMEX una empresa líder mundial en materiales de construcción en seis países diferentes. Ulrick tiene un MBA del MIT Sloan y una licenciatura en ciencias del Tec de Monterrey.

//

Ulrick, is Managing Director at The Entrepreneurship Institute for Monterrey Tech, the most prestigious private university in Latin America and ranked 5th in the world for entrepreneurship. Before he was Digital Transformation and Innovation Lead for Deloitte Consulting, providing services to C-Level Executives across Latin America, Managing Partner at Everdeen Capital, a boutique investment bank, and strategic investment fund; Ulrick is also a seasoned Endeavor Mentor, a dedicated senior lecturer at several graduate schools and serves as board member in several companies and NGOs.

Previously led as CEO and Co-Founder Rocket Internet in Latin America, launching Linio.com and Easy Taxi. Ulrick also led Venture Institute, a premier startup accelerator, as Managing Partner and served as General Manager for Latin American Bridge by Coca-Cola. He has invested in different Venture Capital Funds and as well as Angel Investor. He also has started more than 14 companies across different industries. Ulrick served as manager for Bain & Company and A.T. Kearney, leading strategy consulting firms; and CEMEX a leading global building materials company in six different countries. Ulrick has an MBA from MIT Sloan and bachelor’s in science from Tec de Monterrey.

Tiene experiencia gestionando y liderando equipos, así como promoviendo la colaboración entre instituciones, organizaciones e individuos en la frontera de la inversión, la innovación y la inclusión social y económica.

//

Over 20 years of experience, including extensive field experience, in programs supporting digital innovation, impact investing, public policy advancement, ecosystem building and entrepreneurship, private sector development and innovative finance. PhD in Economics and additional advanced academic credentials in both economics and law. Experience managing and leading teams and promoting collaboration among institutions, organizations and individuals in the frontier of investment, innovation and social and economic inclusion.

speakers.name: Jeffrey Abbott
speakers.bio:
Jeffrey Abbott es el fundador de la comunidad global AI Salon, que apoya a startups y profesionales de IA en 70 ciudades alrededor del mundo. También es autor de AI y el Arte de Ser Humano, un libro que describe una manera de prosperar en la era de la IA mientras redescubrimos aquello que nos hace únicos como seres humanos. Abbott es socio fundador de la firma de capital de riesgo Blitzscaling Ventures, con sede en San Francisco, y fue Profesor Visitante Extranjero de Negocios Internacionales en el ITESM.

//

Jeffrey Abbott is the founder of the AI Salon global community, which supports AI startups and practitioners in 70 cities around the world. He’s also the author of AI and the Art of Being Human, a book that describes a way to thrive in the age of AI while rediscovering what makes us uniquely human. Abbott is a Founding Partner of San Francisco based VC firm Blitzscaling Ventures, and a former Visiting Foreign Professor of International Business at the ITESM.

events.name: The marketing of the future is human: How to differentiate yourself when AI makes everything "generic"?
events.description:
In an environment where artificial intelligence has democratized content production and accelerated campaign execution, the real challenge is no longer "doing more marketing," but rather being more relevant. This keynote proposes a central idea: when everything can be automated, what's scarce is the human touch applied to brand design, experience, and relationships.

Over 20 minutes, Ronan Massez presents a practical framework for leaders and entrepreneurs to use AI as an accelerator without falling into homogenization: what to automate, what to never delegate (empathy, judgment, and consistency), and how to build differentiation based on trust, emotion, and consistent experience. The result: a clear guide to moving from data-driven to human-driven marketing, capable of generating sustainable growth and reputation in the age of AI.
events.location: TEC | INSPIRE STAGE | (RECTORIA - SALA MAYOR)

speakers.name: Ronan Massez
speakers.bio:
Es una de las voces más influyentes en Latinoamérica en temas de marketing, innovación, liderazgo y transformación digital con enfoque humano. Con más de veinte años de experiencia en multinacionales como Mars, Johnson & Johnson, Bayer y GSK, hoy comparte ese conocimiento como conferencista internacional, profesor en el Tecnológico de Monterrey, ADEN Business School e ISDI, además de participar como consultor estratégico en Birth Group. Su lema Sharing is caring refleja su misión: ayudar a empresas y líderes a reconectarse con lo que realmente importa. Creador de nuevos marcos estratégicos, Ronan ha inspirado a miles de profesionales a diseñar estrategias más empáticas, relevantes y sostenibles

//

He is one of the most influential voices in Latin America on marketing, innovation, leadership, and human-centered digital transformation. With over twenty years of experience at multinational companies such as Mars, Johnson & Johnson, Bayer, and GSK, he now shares that knowledge as an international speaker, professor at Tecnológico de Monterrey, ADEN Business School, and ISDI, and as a strategic consultant at Birth Group. His motto, "Sharing is caring," reflects his mission: to help companies and leaders reconnect with what truly matters. A creator of new strategic frameworks, Ronan has inspired thousands of professionals to design more empathetic, relevant, and sustainable strategies.

events.name: From Chaos to Calm: Design Your Semester with Gemini
events.description:
Stop surviving college and start designing it. Have you ever felt like the semester is "eating you alive"? Between 50-page PDFs, finals, and exhaustion, it's easy to lose control. But what if I told you that Artificial Intelligence isn't just for doing assignments, but for giving you back your time?

In this 60-minute workshop, we'll turn Gemini and NotebookLM into your productivity empowerment squad. It's not a tech course; it's your opportunity to discover different tools and use cases for applying AI responsibly and creatively.

speakers.name: Ingrid Avilés Ahued
speakers.position: Líder GTM, Gemini para Educación México, Centroamérica y el Caribe.
speakers.bio:
Líder en Tecnología, Innovación y Adopción Responsable de IA

Con más de 18 años de experiencia y un enfoque en pensamiento sistémico, soy una líder orientada a propósito con una sólida trayectoria gestionando programas globales y regionales en colaboración con gobiernos, ONGs y empresas de primer nivel.

Actualmente, lidero la adopción responsable de Inteligencia Artificial de Google en universidades de México, Centroamérica y el Caribe para el equipo de Google for Education. Previamente, como Country Manager de Waze, dirigí alianzas público-privadas para mejorar la movilidad urbana y lancé soluciones sostenibles como Waze Carpool. En Mercado Libre, fui pieza clave en el posicionamiento de retail media y el lanzamiento de su unidad de Supermercado.

Logros y Reconocimientos
Reconocida entre las 100 Mujeres más Poderosas de los Negocios en México por Expansión (2022, 2023).

Galardonada con el Future is Female Award en Advertising Week LATAM 2024.

Mentora en ecosistemas de emprendimiento como Google for Startups, Endeavor y Laboratoria.

Formación Académica
Soy graduada del Tec de Monterrey en la carrera de Negocios Internacionales y cuento con una maestría de la London School of Economics (LSE), además de estudios especializados en Innovación (MIT), Economía Circular (Berkeley) y Economía del Comportamiento (Yale). Soy Coach Profesional certificada por el Designing Your Life Institute (Stanford).

//

With over 18 years of experience and a systems-thinking approach, I am a purpose-driven leader with a proven record of managing cross-functional, regional, and global programs in collaboration with governments, institutions, NGOs, and top companies.

Recognized among the 100 Most Powerful Women in Business in Mexico by Expansión MX (2022 and 2023), I was also honored with the Future is Female Award at Advertising Week LATAM 2024, which globally celebrates outstanding and pioneering women in the industry.

I currently lead the responsible adoption of Google AI within universities in Mexico, Central America, and the Caribbean for the Google for Education team.

As a former Country Manager for Waze, I directed public-private partnerships through the Waze for Cities program—helping governments across Latin America make data-driven decisions to improve urban mobility through access to historical and real-time data. I successfully launched the Waze Carpool app, a more sustainable mobility solution, building alliances with key cities, universities, and institutions such as Coparmex and Concamin, as well as leading companies in Mexico including Cemex, Liverpool, and BBVA. Co-created Waze for Good Global, an initiative that partnered with NGOs and federal governments to address pressing COVID-19 challenges. Additionally, I collaborated on various papers with organizations such as the IDB and the United Nations, focused on building more resilient mobility systems.

During my eight years at Google, I had the opportunity to work closely with leading organizations across sectors such as Tech, Telco, CPG, Banking, and Retail/E-commerce—supporting major companies like AT&T, Amazon, and Walmart in their digital transformation journey.

I led the development of the first Spanish-language Playbook in LATAM to build Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), gathering best practices from Women@Google and other tech companies. Published on the Google Blog in partnership with Mujeres en Tech.

I was part of the leadership team at Mercado Libre, representing Mercado Libre Ads—one of the organization’s strategic business units—focused on positioning the company as the leader in retail media and driving evangelization and exponential ad investment growth across Latin America reaching 1B USD. I lead the launch of its Supermarket unit, a strategic pillar to drive purchase frequency, partnering with major CPGs such as Nestlé, Coca-Cola, Unilever, Mondelez, Diageo, ADM, Mars, Hills, Sanofi and Colgate and the largest brands in the beauty industry: L'Oreal, BDF, Isdin etc.

Beyond my experience in the tech industry, I have developed deep expertise in foreign trade consulting, international supply chain management, and ERP implementation through my roles at consulting and retail companies such as PwC and El Palacio de Hierro.

I serve as a mentor for various impact-driven entrepreneurship programs and organizations, including Google for Startups, Impact Hub, Endeavor, Lucha, Amela, Laboratoria, Mujeres en Tech, Great Latinas for Good, and the Miller Center—seeking innovative ways to create greater impact for the greater good.

I hold a degree in International Business from Tec de Monterrey and a Master’s degree from the London School of Economics, as well as further studies in Circular Economy (Berkeley), Innovation (MIT), Customer Analytics (Wharton), and Behavioral Economics (Yale).

I lead my own professional coaching practice and hold a certification from the Designing Your Life Institute, an initiative from Stanford.

events.name: Slow Down to Speed Up: The AI Paradox in Regulated Industries
events.description: How can you leverage accelerated innovation in highly regulated environments where there is no room for error? In this session, you’ll discover why security isn't a bottleneck, but rather the engine behind AI. We will analyze strategies for scaling models with confidence, transforming regulation into a competitive advantage, and exploring real-world strategic execution lessons to navigate this ecosystem.

speakers.name: Christian González
speakers.bio:
Christian es Manager en Capital One, donde lidera proyectos de modernización de datos. Con más de 10 años de experiencia en desarrollo de software (EdTech y MarTech), su trayectoria destaca por ser poco convencional: inició como emprendedor autodidacta tras estudiar Ciencia Política, para luego profesionalizarse con una Maestría en Computer Science por Georgia Tech.

Su perfil se enriquece con experiencias como profesor de primaria, auditor gubernamental y fundador de una escuela de salsa cubana. Hoy, utiliza esta combinación de ciencias sociales y agilidad emprendedora para construir equipos innovadores, siempre listos para responder a las preguntas más importantes.

//

Christian is a Manager at Capital One, where he leads data modernization projects. With over 10 years of experience in software development (EdTech and MarTech), his career path stands out for being unconventional: he started as a self-taught entrepreneur after studying Political Science, and later formalized his expertise with a Master's in Computer Science from Georgia Tech.

His profile is further enriched by his past experiences as an elementary school teacher, a government auditor, and the founder of a Cuban salsa school. Today, he uses this blend of social sciences and entrepreneurial agility to build innovative teams, always ready to answer the most important questions.

speakers.name: Iván Montoya
speakers.bio:
Ivan Montoya es socio fundador de NuMundo Ventures, un fondo de capital semilla que apoya a emprendedores excepcionales en América Latina y Estados Unidos.

Ivan ha invertido en más de 50 startups en América Latina, incluyendo empresas emergentes de gran éxito como Cashea y OCN.

Nacido en Colombia y residente en Silicon Valley, Ivan cuenta con una amplia red de contactos en la comunidad de startups latinoamericanas y adopta un enfoque práctico y personalizado para evaluar y apoyar a los emprendedores.

Anteriormente, Ivan trabajó en empresas tecnológicas respaldadas por capital de riesgo, en McKinsey, y cuenta con un MBA de Stanford y una licenciatura de Harvard.

//

Ivan Montoya is the founding general partner of NuMundo Ventures, a seed stage fund backing exceptional founders in Latin America and the US.

Ivan has invested in over 50 startups in Latin America including early breakout companies like Cashea and OCN.

Born in Colombia and based in Silicon Valley, Ivan is hyper connected in the LatAM startup community and takes a unique hands-on approach to evaluate and back founders.

Earlier in his career, Ivan was an operator in venture backed technology companies, worked at McKinsey, and has an MBA from Stanford and a BA from Harvard.

speakers.name: Luis Mario García
speakers.bio:
Luis es el CEO y cofundador de Seals.ai, una empresa que ayuda a mayoristas y distribuidores a automatizar sus operaciones.

Esta es la segunda vez que Luis funda una empresa, tras haber llevado su anterior startup, Orchata, una empresa de comercio rápido, a alcanzar 6 millones de dólares en ingresos y 30.000 clientes.

Luis ha participado en Y Combinator en dos ocasiones y es egresado del Tecnológico de Monterrey.

//

Luis is the CEO and co-founder of Seals.ai which hleps wholesalers and distributors automate their operations.

Luis is second time founder having grown his previous startup Orchata, a quick commerce company, to $6M in revenue and 30K customers.

Luis is a two time YC founder and graduate of Tecnológico de Monterrey.

speakers.name: Luis Olave
speakers.bio:
Luis Olave es el CEO y cofundador de Altur, una empresa que utiliza inteligencia artificial de voz para la gestión de cobro de deudas.

Instituciones financieras y centros de llamadas en todo México utilizan los productos de Altur.

Luis es fundador de una empresa que participó en Y Combinator y es egresado del Tecnológico de Monterrey.

//

Luis Olave is the CEO and co-founder of Altur which uses voice AI to for debt collection.

Financial instituions and call centers use Altur's products across all of Mexico.

Luis is aY Combinator founder and graduate of Tecnológico de Monterrey.

speakers.name: Mauricio Valdes
speakers.bio:
Mauricio Valdés es ingeniero civil egresado del Tecnológico de Monterrey y fundador de Buildpeer, una startup de tecnología para la construcción que está revolucionando la gestión de proyectos en la industria de la construcción mexicana.

Mientras trabajaba en proyectos de construcción en Estados Unidos y México, Mauricio identificó las ineficiencias en la comunicación y la gestión de proyectos que afectan al sector de la construcción en México.

En 2022, fundó Buildpeer para ofrecer una plataforma intuitiva y fácil de usar que empodera a los equipos de construcción desde el primer día.

//

Mauricio Valdés is a civil engineer who graduated from Tecnológico de Monterrey and the founder of Buildpeer, a construction technology startup that is revolutionizing project management in the Mexican construction industry.

While working on construction projects in the United States and Mexico, Mauricio identified the inefficiencies in communication and project management that plague the construction sector in Mexico.

In 2022, he founded Buildpeer to provide an intuitive and user-friendly platform that empowers construction teams from day one.

events.name: Designed to Bloom
events.description: In a world where artificial intelligence is accelerating the future, Designed to Flourish reminds us that the greatest advancement remains human. By discovering and transforming our talents, we align creativity and technology to deliver relevant, authentic, and essential contributions to the evolution of our society.
events.location: TEC | INSPIRE STAGE | (RECTORIA - SALA MAYOR)

speakers.name: Rosemary Martínez Jover
speakers.position: Creative Director &. Founder / Directora Creativa y Fundadora
speakers.bio:
Rosemary Martínez es una creadora indomable: diseñadora multidisciplinaria, disruptiva, maestra en innovación, escritora y conferencista internacional dedicada al despertar creativo como base del florecimiento humano.

Con más de cuarenta años de trayectoria, ha detonado su creatividad para concebir y desarrollar proyectos emblemáticos —restaurantes icónicos y desarrollos inmobiliarios visionarios— reconocidos y premiados nacional e internacionalmente. Ha recibido distinciones como el Premio Rufino Tamayo, el premio Mérito Ciudadano y el Premio a la Trayectoria profesional, consolidándola como una fuerza creativa que transforma empresas, personas y comunidades.

Ha llevado su mensaje a la ONU, a dos TEDx, y a giras por México, Centroamérica y España, inspirando a miles con su visión sobre creatividad, innovación y transformación.

Autora del bestseller Adiós mamá, adiós papá y del libro Bloom, hoy desarrolla dos nuevas obras que expanden su filosofía de evolución personal y colectiva.

Rosemary nos recuerda que el futuro florece cuando cada individuo descubre su talento y se atreve a ponerlo a servicio del mundo.

//

Rosemary Martínez is a passionate and unstoppable designer, innovation expert, author and keynote speaker committed to sparking creativity as the source of true growth and human transformation.

For over forty years, she has brought her creativity to life through emblematic projects—from iconic restaurants to visionary real-estate developments—recognized and awarded both nationally and internationally. She has received honors such as the Rufino Tamayo Award, the Citizen Merit Award, and The design Achievement Award, among many others, positioning herself as a creative force that transforms companies, people, and communities.

She has shared her message at the United Nations, on two TEDx stages, and throughout Mexico, Central America, and Spain, inspiring thousands with her perspective on creativity, innovation, and personal transformation.

She is the author of the Amazon bestseller Good bye Mum, Good Bye Dad and Bloom, wherever you may be planted, and is currently working on two new books that expand her philosophy of personal and collective evolution.

Rosemary reminds us that the future blooms when each one of us discovers our unique talents—and dares to share them with the world.

events.name: Book meeting: The BIG 3
events.description:
In The BIG 3, Efraín Mendicuti dismantles, with lucidity, humor, and brutal honesty, one of the great myths of the modern world: that doing more equals getting further. Drawing on his experience leading teams at global companies like Google, Twitter, and Pinterest, and years of mentoring leaders, executives, and entrepreneurs, the author proposes a methodology as simple as it is powerful: choosing just three top priorities to guide your energy, your decisions, and your life.

This is not a traditional productivity book or a collection of empty formulas. It's a compass. The BIG 3 invites you to stop being trapped by pending tasks, other people's urgent needs, and endless lists, and to start acting with intention, purpose, and clarity. Through real-life stories, profound reflections, and practical tools, Mendicuti shows how focusing on what's important—at work and in your personal life—can transform results, well-being, and your sense of direction.

With a warm, ironic, and deeply human tone, the author explores themes such as purpose, non-negotiable values, rest as a high-performance strategy, the difference between movement and progress, and the power of saying “no” without guilt. The result is a method applicable to both organizations and individuals seeking more than just success: they seek coherence. The BIG 3 doesn't promise magic. It promises something better: clarity to choose, discipline to move forward, and awareness to build a life, and a year, that are truly worthwhile.
events.location: TEC | (EXPEDITION FEMSA - VENTURE CAFE)

speakers.name: Efraín Mendicuti
speakers.position: Director General de Negocios para México y los mercados de habla hispana en Latinoamérica
speakers.bio:
Efraín Mendicuti es un líder visionario en marketing digital y transformación empresarial con más de 30 años de trayectoria impulsando la evolución del ecosistema digital en México y América Latina. Su legado se ha forjado en el corazón de las plataformas más influyentes del mundo: fue parte del equipo fundador de Google en México, contribuyó a establecer y expandir la operación comercial de Twitter para América Latina y lideró el lanzamiento de TikTok for Business en México. Hoy dirige el negocio de Pinterest para México y los mercados de habla hispana en América Latina, donde continúa transformando la forma en que las marcas conectan con las personas a través de la inspiración y la creatividad digital.

Reconocido por su liderazgo, compromiso con el desarrollo de talento y aporte a la profesionalización de la industria, Mendicuti fue distinguido como una de las 20 figuras clave que han marcado la transformación digital del marketing y la publicidad en México por IAB México en su 20º aniversario, reflejo de su impacto y relevancia en los últimos años.

Con formación en Comunicación y Publicidad por la Universidad Anáhuac, con estudios en liderazgo y gestión de talento en Harvard Business School y Yale School of Management, Mendicuti ha ampliado su influencia más allá de la práctica empresarial. Es coach ejecutivo y corporativo, conferencista internacional y TEDx Speaker, reconocido por integrar la conciencia humana en la conducción de estrategias y equipos de alto rendimiento. Además de su rol empresarial, ha desempeñado funciones docentes en programas de Maestría y Posgrado en ISDI y la Universidad Anáhuac, y ha creado el primer diplomado de Marketing Digital Integral de esa casa de estudios. También es conductor del podcast EntreMentorES #DLC, donde explora historias de aprendizaje y liderazgo con voces influyentes de múltiples industrias.

En su faceta como autor de Los GRANDES 3, condensa en esta obra más de treinta años de experiencia acompañando a personas y líderes en la búsqueda de claridad y propósito, proponiendo un modelo práctico para gobernar el mundo interior y transformar la vida exterior con conciencia y enfoque.

Mendicuti es reconocido por su experiencia técnica y enfoque humanista: cree en el liderazgo con propósito, en la priorización consciente y en la construcción de culturas organizacionales que celebren la creatividad, la colaboración y la curiosidad como motores de cambio en un mundo digital en constante reinvención.

//

Efraín Mendicuti is a visionary leader in digital marketing and business transformation with over 30 years of experience driving the evolution of the digital ecosystem in Mexico and Latin America. His legacy has been forged at the heart of the world’s most influential platforms: he was part of the founding team of Google in Mexico, helped establish and expand Twitter’s commercial operations for Latin America, and led the launch of TikTok for Business in Mexico. Currently, he heads the business for Pinterest in Mexico and Spanish-speaking Latin American markets, where he continues to transform how brands connect with audiences through inspiration and digital creativity.

Recognized for his leadership, commitment to talent development, and contribution to the professionalization of the industry, Mendicuti was honored by IAB Mexico on its 20th anniversary as one of the 20 key figures who have defined the digital transformation of marketing and advertising in the country—a testament to his enduring impact and relevance.

With a degree in Communication and Advertising from Universidad Anáhuac, and executive studies in leadership and talent management from Harvard Business School and Yale School of Management, Mendicuti has expanded his influence far beyond the boardroom. He is an executive and corporate coach, international keynote speaker, and TEDx Speaker, recognized for integrating human consciousness into the management of high-performance strategies and teams.

In addition to his corporate roles, he has served as a professor for Master’s and Postgraduate programs at ISDI and Universidad Anáhuac, where he created the university’s first Comprehensive Digital Marketing diploma. He is also the host of the podcast EntreMentorES #DLC, where he explores stories of learning and leadership with influential voices across multiple industries.

In his role as an author, his book Los GRANDES 3 (The Big 3) condenses over three decades of experience guiding individuals and leaders in their search for clarity and purpose. The work proposes a practical model for mastering one’s inner world to transform external life with awareness and focus.

Mendicuti is celebrated for both his technical expertise and his humanistic approach. He is a firm believer in purpose-driven leadership, conscious prioritization, and building organizational cultures that celebrate creativity, collaboration, and curiosity as the primary engines of change in a constantly reinventing digital world.

events.name: SME Day: Graduation of the Free SME SOS Digitalization Program
events.description:
SME Day, organized by the Center of Excellence for SME Business Development at Tecnológico de Monterrey, is a space for learning and business connection where the national community of small and medium-sized business owners gathers at INC Monterrey.
In this edition, we will celebrate the graduation of the free SME SOS Digitalization Program, joined by Betsabé Rocha, Secretary of Economy of the Government of the State of Nuevo León. In addition, we will host e-commerce workshops with the Argentine unicorn Tiendanube, explore the impact of artificial intelligence through a workshop by Lovable, and learn about the global opportunities available to SMEs through the international network of Small Business Development Centers (SBDC) in the United States.
Note: The event will take place from 2:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. on the second floor of the Expedition Entrepreneurship Gateway building.

speakers.name: Mitra Miller
speakers.company: Mill River Capital / Houston Angel Network
speakers.bio:
Mitra Miller es una inversionista, estratega y asesora con amplia experiencia, dedicada a impulsar empresas de alto crecimiento a la vanguardia de la innovación. Como socia directora de Mill River Capital, pone al servicio de fundadores e inversionistas sus 25 años de trayectoria para brindar asesoría en planeación de negocios, comercialización, capitalización, operaciones y procesos de due diligence.

La Sra. Miller también se desempeña como presidenta y miembro del consejo de la Houston Angel Network, uno de los grupos de inversionistas en etapa temprana más activos de Estados Unidos, que invierte en startups en fases pre-seed, seed y Serie A dentro de los sectores de energía, tecnología, ciencias de la vida, consumo y aeroespacial.

Oradora dinámica y autora publicada, asesora a organizaciones públicas, privadas y sin fines de lucro en la formación de capital y el desarrollo de ecosistemas de innovación, además de formar parte de varios consejos enfocados en ampliar las oportunidades para comunidades con recursos limitados.

//

Mitra Miller is an experienced investor, strategist, and advisor dedicated to building high-growth companies at the forefront of innovation. As the managing partner of Mill River Capital, she uses her 25 years of experience to advise founders and investors on business planning, commercialization, capitalization, operations, and due diligence. Ms. Miller also serves as President and Board Member of the Houston Angel Network, one of the most active early-stage investor groups in the U.S., which invests in pre-seed, seed, and Series A ventures in the energy, technology, life sciences, consumer, and aerospace sectors. A dynamic speaker and published author, she advises public, private, and nonprofit organizations on capital formation and developing innovation ecosystems, and she serves on the boards of multiple organizations focused on expanding opportunities for communities with limited resources.

speakers.name: Rosemary Martínez Jover
speakers.position: Creative Director &. Founder / Directora Creativa y Fundadora
speakers.bio:
Rosemary Martínez es una creadora indomable: diseñadora multidisciplinaria, disruptiva, maestra en innovación, escritora y conferencista internacional dedicada al despertar creativo como base del florecimiento humano.

Con más de cuarenta años de trayectoria, ha detonado su creatividad para concebir y desarrollar proyectos emblemáticos —restaurantes icónicos y desarrollos inmobiliarios visionarios— reconocidos y premiados nacional e internacionalmente. Ha recibido distinciones como el Premio Rufino Tamayo, el premio Mérito Ciudadano y el Premio a la Trayectoria profesional, consolidándola como una fuerza creativa que transforma empresas, personas y comunidades.

Ha llevado su mensaje a la ONU, a dos TEDx, y a giras por México, Centroamérica y España, inspirando a miles con su visión sobre creatividad, innovación y transformación.

Autora del bestseller Adiós mamá, adiós papá y del libro Bloom, hoy desarrolla dos nuevas obras que expanden su filosofía de evolución personal y colectiva.

Rosemary nos recuerda que el futuro florece cuando cada individuo descubre su talento y se atreve a ponerlo a servicio del mundo.

//

She has shared her message at the United Nations, on two TEDx stages, and throughout Mexico, Central America, and Spain, inspiring thousands with her perspective on creativity, innovation, and personal transformation.

She is the author of the Amazon bestseller Good bye Mum, Good Bye Dad and Bloom, wherever you may be planted, and is currently working on two new books that expand her philosophy of personal and collective evolution.

Rosemary reminds us that the future blooms when each one of us discovers our unique talents—and dares to share them with the world.

events.name: Let's Speak Our Language: From 'Finance Bro' to Finance for All
events.description:
Have you ever felt like the investment world doesn't speak your language? It's no coincidence: the system was designed to exclude us.
In this talk, we explore how financial jargon became a barrier and how we embarked on a journey to hack it. From a system designed for the few, "Bro-talk," to a clearer, more human, and transparent way of investing. That's why Pilou was born: to transform investing into a tool for freedom and empowerment.
events.location: TEC | CREATE WORKSHOPS | (BIBLIOTECA - SALA POEMA & DIALOGO)

speakers.name: Andrea de la Garza
speakers.bio:
Andrea, fundadora y CEO de Pilou, ha dedicado más de 16 años de su vida en el sector financiero. En el 2020 fundó Pilou, una plataforma de ahorro e inversión para todos los latinos. Trabajó más de 8 años en Credit Suisse y más de 2 en Grupo Bursátil Mexicano (GBM).

Además, combina su experiencia financiera con una pasión por el impacto social, liderando proyectos en Fundación Creando Cambios y Fundación Sapiencia. Graduada del ITESM Campus Monterrey y con un diplomado de Numa, Andrea fue reconocida como embajadora de One Young World para México en 2019.

//

Andrea, founder and CEO of Pilou, has been over 16 years in the financial sector. In 2020, she founded Pilou, a savings and investment platform designed to make investing more accessible for people across Latin America. She previously worked for more than eight years at Credit Suisse and over two years at Grupo Bursátil Mexicano (GBM).

Alongside her financial career, Andrea is deeply committed to social impact. She has led initiatives with Fundación Creando Cambios and Fundación Sapiencia, integrating her experience with purpose. Andrea graduated from ITESM Campus Monterrey and completed an entrepreneurship diploma at Numa. In 2019, she was recognized as a One Young World Ambassador for Mexico.

speakers.name: Juliana Garaizar
speakers.bio:
Juliana Garaizar es una reconocida inversora de capital de riesgo, líder y promotora del ecosistema emprendedor, con sede principalmente en Houston, Texas. Es conocida por su trabajo impulsando fundadores diversos y empresas de tecnología climática.

Inversora y líder en venture capital: Juliana es socia general e inversora principal en Portfolia, donde trabaja en fondos como Rising America Fund, enfocados en apoyar a fundadores latinos.

Ejecutiva en tecnología limpia e innovación: Fue Chief Development and Investment Officer en Greentown Labs, uno de los principales incubadores de tecnología climática, y desempeñó un papel clave en el desarrollo de su sede en Houston. Ahora es Partner en Greenbackers Investment Capital.

Roles en consejos y asesorías: Formo parte del Consejo de la Angel Capital Association y colabora en múltiples consejos asesores que apoyan la diversidad y el emprendimiento.

Amplia experiencia en liderazgo: A lo largo de su carrera ha dirigido el Texas Medical Center Venture Fund, liderado el Houston Angel Network y gestionado redes internacionales de inversionistas ángeles en Europa y globalmente.

Trayectoria global y multidisciplinaria: Inició su carrera en Singapur trabajando con la Oficina Comercial de la Embajada de España y Citigroup. Cuenta con un doble MBA de London Business School y Haas School of Business (UC Berkeley), con enfoque en emprendimiento.

//

Juliana Garaizar is a well-known venture capital investor, leader, and champion of the entrepreneurial ecosystem, based primarily in Houston, Texas. She is recognized for her work supporting diverse founders and climate technology companies.

Investor and venture capital leader: Juliana is a General Partner and lead investor at Portfolia, where she works on funds such as the Rising America Fund, focused on supporting Latino founders and other underrepresented entrepreneurs.

Clean technology and innovation executive: She previously served as Chief Development and Investment Officer at Greentown Labs, one of the world’s leading climate technology incubators, where she played a key role in the development and launch of its Houston location. She is currently a Partner at Greenbackers Investment Capital.

Board and advisory roles: She is a member of the Board of the Angel Capital Association and collaborates with multiple advisory boards that promote diversity, innovation, and entrepreneurship.

Extensive leadership experience: Throughout her career, she has led the Texas Medical Center Venture Fund, headed the Houston Angel Network, and managed international angel investor networks across Europe and globally.

Global and multidisciplinary background: She began her career in Singapore working with the Commercial Office of the Spanish Embassy and with Citigroup. She holds a dual MBA from London Business School and the Haas School of Business (UC Berkeley), with a focus on entrepreneurship.

Here and Now: Training Attention
A 25-minute in-person practice, based on the Healthy Minds program and developed by the Tecmilenio Institute of Purpose and Integral Wellbeing, that invites you to pause the daily rhythm and train mindfulness through guided breathing exercises and awareness of the present moment.

speakers.name: Epifanio Sánchez
speakers.bio:
Licenciado en Administración de Empresas por el Tecnológico de Monterrey, institución en la que se distinguió por su desempeño académico y liderazgo estudiantil. Durante su etapa como estudiante y a lo largo de varios años, fue columnista semanal de opinión en los periódicos El Norte (Monterrey) y Reforma (Ciudad de México), compartiendo espacio con reconocidas figuras del ámbito intelectual y cultural como Carlos Fuentes, Armando Fuentes Aguirre ?Catón? y Germán Dehesa.

Su trayectoria profesional incluye experiencia en los sectores de relaciones públicas, tecnologías de la información y finanzas. Asimismo, durante 15 años se desempeñó como emprendedor en las industrias de la construcción y la energía, periodo en el cual obtuvo una patente registrada a su nombre.

Desde hace más de una década, ha orientado su trayectoria profesional al desarrollo humano y a su integración con la gestión empresarial. Cuenta con una Maestría en Psicología Positiva Aplicada, así como una Maestría en Neuropsicología, Inteligencias Múltiples y Mindfulness.

//

He holds a Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration from Tecnológico de Monterrey, where he distinguished himself through academic excellence and student leadership. During his years as a student and for several years thereafter, he served as a weekly opinion columnist for the newspapers El Norte (Monterrey) and Reforma (Mexico City), sharing space with renowned intellectual and cultural figures such as Carlos Fuentes, Armando Fuentes Aguirre (“Catón”), and Germán Dehesa.

His professional career includes experience in the public relations, information technology, and finance sectors. Additionally, he worked for 15 years as an entrepreneur in the construction and energy industries, during which time he obtained a patent registered in his name.

For more than a decade, he has focused his professional career on human development and its integration with business management. He holds a Master’s degree in Applied Positive Psychology, as well as a Master’s degree in Neuropsychology, Multiple Intelligences, and Mindfulness.

events.name: Bookmeeting: From consumer to ‘homo experiens’: trends in experience and emotion
events.description:
In a market where technology and AI are accelerating campaigns, content, and automation, the true differentiator is no longer just data: it's experience. This talk explores the paradigm shift from the "rational consumer" to homo experiens: a conscious, emotional, and multidimensional individual who chooses brands based on how they make them feel, the coherence of their experiences, and the meaning they construct in every interaction.

Based on current trends in experience, emotion, and behavior, the session presents how journeys are evolving (becoming more fragmented and "phygital"), why emotion has become a strategic asset (memory, preference, recommendation), and what this means for designing relevant brands: less interruption, more utility; less promise, more proof; less campaigning, more consistency. The content culminates in a practical framework for leaders and entrepreneurs to identify "moments of truth" and redesign experiences that generate real differentiation, trust, and growth.
events.location: TEC | (EXPEDITION FEMSA - VENTURE CAFE)

speakers.name: Ronan Massez
speakers.bio:
Es una de las voces más influyentes en Latinoamérica en temas de marketing, innovación, liderazgo y transformación digital con enfoque humano. Con más de veinte años de experiencia en multinacionales como Mars, Johnson & Johnson, Bayer y GSK, hoy comparte ese conocimiento como conferencista internacional, profesor en el Tecnológico de Monterrey, ADEN Business School e ISDI, además de participar como consultor estratégico en Birth Group. Su lema Sharing is caring refleja su misión: ayudar a empresas y líderes a reconectarse con lo que realmente importa. Creador de nuevos marcos estratégicos, Ronan ha inspirado a miles de profesionales a diseñar estrategias más empáticas, relevantes y sostenibles

//

He is one of the most influential voices in Latin America on marketing, innovation, leadership, and human-centered digital transformation. With over twenty years of experience at multinational companies such as Mars, Johnson & Johnson, Bayer, and GSK, he now shares that knowledge as an international speaker, professor at Tecnológico de Monterrey, ADEN Business School, and ISDI, and as a strategic consultant at Birth Group. His motto, "Sharing is caring," reflects his mission: to help companies and leaders reconnect with what truly matters. A creator of new strategic frameworks, Ronan has inspired thousands of professionals to design more empathetic, relevant, and sustainable strategies.

events.name: Unwavering Leadership
events.description:
Unwavering Leadership

In an environment where innovation moves faster than certainty, leadership is no longer based solely on strategy, but on the mental fortitude and human skills of the leader.

In this conference, we will explore how truly unwavering leadership is forged, not innate. Being a leader today is not just about directing; it's about remaining calm when everything changes, making conscious decisions, and inspiring others to go beyond what they thought possible.

Because true leadership emerges when your mind becomes unwavering…
and your team perceives it.
events.location: TEC | INNOVATION WORKSHOP | (EXPEDITION FEMSA - SALA POLIVALENTE)

speakers.name: Celia Martínez del Toro
speakers.bio:
Celia Martínez del Toro es Directora Comercial en Marcon Industrial, empresaria en el sector de la construcción y Co fundadora de Kazta Consultores, empresa dedicada a Institucionalizar Empresas Familiares.

Cuenta con una especialidad en Interiorismo por ELLE Decoration de la Universidad Complutense de Madrid, así como un Diplomado en Empresas Familiares por el IPADE Business School. Su sólida formación se complementa con una trayectoria destacada en el ámbito empresarial y el desarrollo organizacional.

Conferencista especialista en el desarrollo de habilidades humanas, liderazgo estratégico y dinámicas en empresas familiares. Es una voz activa en foros empresariales en temas de liderazgo, resiliencia y gestión emocional para líderes de alto impacto. Su enfoque integra profundidad estratégica y claridad emocional, ayudando a transformar el miedo en impulso y los desafíos en oportunidades reales de crecimiento.

En 2024 fue reconocida con el Premio Internacional Mujer Empresaria otorgado por CONCANACO, así como con el premio “Construyendo la Excelencia” de CEMEX, gracias a su liderazgo como Directora de Operaciones en un proyecto de Marcon Industrial.

//

Celia Martínez del Toro is Commercial Director at Marcon Industrial, an entrepreneur in the construction sector, and Co-Founder of Kazta Consultores, a firm dedicated to the institutionalization of family-owned businesses.

She holds a specialization in Interior Design from ELLE Decoration at Complutense University of Madrid, as well as a Diploma in Family Business Management from IPADE Business School. Her solid academic background is complemented by a distinguished career in business leadership and organizational development.

Celia is a keynote speaker specializing in human skills development, strategic leadership, and family business dynamics. She is an active voice in business forums, addressing topics such as leadership, resilience, and emotional management for high-impact leaders. Her approach combines strategic depth with emotional clarity, helping leaders transform fear into momentum and challenges into real opportunities for growth.

In 2024, she was honored with the International Businesswoman Award granted by CONCANACO, as well as the “Building Excellence” Award from CEMEX, in recognition of her leadership as Operations Director on a Marcon Industrial project.

events.name: Everyone can change the world
events.description:
In his talk, "Everyone Can Change the World," young social entrepreneur Mateo Salvatto guides us through his journey from an average high school student to one of Argentina's most respected tech entrepreneurs.

Mateo takes us from laughter to tears, showing us how his projects (with their successes and failures) can help empower more than 500,000 people with disabilities worldwide, and convinces us that changing the world is much easier than we think.
events.location: TEC | TELCEL 5G STAGE | (CENTRO DE CONGRESOS - SALA 1 Y 2)

speakers.name: Mateo Salvatto
speakers.bio:
Mateo Salvatto, MBA (IAE Business School), es un emprendedor tecnológico de 26 años originario de Buenos Aires, Argentina. A los 17, fue Campeón Internacional de Robótica y un año después creó su primera empresa: Asteroid, cuyo primer producto, Háblalo, ya asiste gratuitamente a más de 500.000 personas con discapacidad en todo el mundo y transforma más de 30 organizaciones en espacios accesibles.

Mateo también es Director de Innovación en ORT Schools, presentador del podcast "10 Minutos para Cambiar el Mundo", autor de los éxitos de ventas "La Batalla del Futuro" y "País de Mierda", y fue reconocido como Innovador Humanitario del Año por MIT Technology Review, Mejor Innovador Social por la Universidad de Pekín y Emprendedor Global del Año por OneYoungWorld en el Reino Unido, entre otros premios. Su empresa, Asteroid, fue seleccionada entre las 100 mejores startups del mundo por la Red Global de Emprendimiento de Arabia Saudita y recientemente recibió inversión de Globant Ventures, el fondo de inversión del unicornio argentino.

También es cofundador de Apgreid, una plataforma de educación digital, y de Grondplek, la primera empresa de construcción aditiva de Latinoamérica.

Recientemente, Mateo fue nombrado por la consultora Poliarquía como el décimo empresario más destacado de Argentina y reconocido como uno de los 200 "Líderes del Mañana" a nivel mundial por el 53.º Simposio de St. Gallen en Suiza. También fue reconocido como "Emprendedor Global del Año" por One Young World en el Reino Unido.

//

Mateo Salvatto, MBA (IAE Business School), is a 26-year-old technology entrepreneur from Buenos Aires, Argentina. At 17, he was International Robotics Champion and a year later he created his first company: Asteroid, whose first product, Háblalo, already assists more than 500,000 people with disabilities around the world for free and transforms more than 30 organizations into accessible spaces.

Mateo is also currently Director of Innovation at ORT Schools, host of the podcast “10 Minutes to Change the World,” author of the best sellers “La Batalla del Futuro” and “País de Mierda,” and was recognized as Humanitarian Innovator of the Year by MIT Technology Review, Best Social Innovator by Peking University, and Global Entrepreneur of the Year by OneYoungWorld in the United Kingdom, among other awards. His company, Asteroid, was chosen among the TOP 100 Startups in the world by the Global Entrepreneurship Network of Saudi Arabia and recently invested by Globant Ventures, the investment fund of the Argentine unicorn.

He is also co-founder of Apgreid, a digital education platform, and of Grondplek, the first additive construction company in Latin America.

Recently, Mateo was named by the consulting firm Poliarquía as the 10th most outstanding businessman in Argentina and recognized as one of the 200 "Leaders of Tomorrow" worldwide by the 53rd St. Gallen Symposium in Switzerland. Also recognized as "Global Entrepreneur of the Year" by One Young World in the UK.

events.name: The new capitals of entrepreneurship
events.description:
Entrepreneurship no longer happens only in major global hubs. In Mexico, a new generation of cities is demonstrating that it's possible to build ecosystems capable of generating global startups, attracting investment, and transforming regional economies.

In this panel, leaders in entrepreneurship, technology, and venture capital will share how communities are being created in Querétaro, Hermosillo, Puebla, Chihuahua, and Aguascalientes that connect talent, capital, universities, and corporations to drive impactful innovation. A conversation about what it takes for more regions to become the new entrepreneurial capitals.
events.location: TEC | BOLD VISION | (EXPEDITION FEMSA - AUDITORIO)

speakers.name: Luis Almanza
speakers.bio:
Construyo y escalo ecosistemas de innovación que impulsan startups de alto impacto, oportunidades de inversión y crecimiento económico. Como Director General en Orion Startups y Director Regional en el Instituto de Emprendimiento Eugenio Garza Lagüera (Tecnológico de Monterrey), integro capital de riesgo, educación en emprendimiento y políticas estratégicas para empoderar a fundadores, inversionistas y líderes corporativos.

Con experiencia comprobada en capital de riesgo, aceleración de startups y diseño de ecosistemas, he liderado fondos de inversión, desarrollado metodologías para startups y construido alianzas intersectoriales que conectan a América Latina con los principales hubs de innovación global. Ya sea asesorando a startups, mentorando a inversionistas o diseñando iniciativas gubernamentales de emprendimiento, disfruto crear un impacto tangible.

Impulsado por una pasión por los sistemas escalables y el crecimiento transformador, me especializo en:

● Capital de riesgo e inversión en startups: Financiamiento en etapas tempranas, gestión de portafolios y estrategias para inversionistas.
● Desarrollo de ecosistemas de innovación: Estructuración de aceleradoras, marcos de políticas y programas de innovación corporativa.
● Educación emprendedora y liderazgo intelectual: Diseño de currículos, mentoría a fundadores y participación como conferencista en foros globales.

Más allá de la inversión y la educación, estoy profundamente comprometido con la creación y fortalecimiento de colaboraciones estratégicas que impulsen la innovación a largo plazo. Mi objetivo es que el ecosistema emprendedor de América Latina no solo sea competitivo, sino también influyente a nivel global, conectando agencias gubernamentales, instituciones académicas e inversionistas privados.

Mi trabajo se basa en la convicción de que la innovación con impacto surge cuando diversos actores se alinean en torno a una visión compartida de transformación económica.

Conectemos y demos forma juntos al futuro del capital de riesgo y el emprendimiento

//

I build and scale innovation ecosystems that drive high-impact startups, investment opportunities, and economic growth. As CEO of Orion Startups and Regional Director at the Eugenio Garza Lagüera Institute for Entrepreneurship (Tecnológico de Monterrey), I integrate venture capital, entrepreneurship education, and strategic policies to empower founders, investors, and corporate leaders.

With proven experience in venture capital, startup acceleration, and ecosystem design, I have led investment funds, developed startup methodologies, and built cross-sector alliances connecting Latin America with the world's leading innovation hubs. Whether advising startups, mentoring investors, or designing government entrepreneurship initiatives, I enjoy creating tangible impact.

Driven by a passion for scalable systems and transformative growth, I specialize in:

● Venture capital and startup investment: Early-stage funding, portfolio management, and investor strategies.

● ● Innovation ecosystem development: Structuring accelerators, policy frameworks, and corporate innovation programs.

● Entrepreneurial education and thought leadership: Curriculum design, mentoring founders, and speaking at global forums.

Beyond investment and education, I am deeply committed to creating and strengthening strategic collaborations that drive long-term innovation. My goal is for Latin America's entrepreneurial ecosystem to be not only competitive but also globally influential, connecting government agencies, academic institutions, and private investors.

My work is based on the conviction that impactful innovation emerges when diverse actors align around a shared vision of economic transformation.

Let's connect and shape the future of venture capital and entrepreneurship together.

speakers.name: Sergio Almaguer
speakers.bio:
Fundador, Emprendedor Digital.

Sergio es cofundador y CEO de Yaydoo y un emprendedor digital con más de 15 años de experiencia en la creación y el crecimiento de empresas tecnológicas con clientes en Latinoamérica, Estados Unidos y Europa.
Ha liderado equipos de Tecnología, Marketing Digital y Ventas en diversas startups y trabajó como Consultor de TI y Gerente de Proyectos para empresas como Microsoft y GE Energy.
Se graduó de la Escuela Politécnica Federal Suiza de Lausana (EPFL) con una Maestría en Gestión de Tecnología y Emprendimiento, y es Licenciado en Tecnologías de la Información por el Tecnológico de Monterrey (ITESM) en México.

Sergio cuenta con una sólida experiencia en la definición y ejecución de estrategias digitales para empresas y startups, y se especializa en la intersección entre Producto y Gestión de la Venta (GTM). Le interesa la implementación de estrategias de crecimiento digital en diferentes industrias.

Sus áreas de especialización incluyen: Capital de Riesgo, Liderazgo Multidisciplinario, Gestión de Producto, Go-to-Market B2B, CRM, Estrategia Digital, Growth Hacking, Comercialización de Tecnología y Ventas Empresariales.

//

Founder, Digital Entrepreneur.

Sergio is co-founder and CEO of Yaydoo and is a digital entrepreneur with 15+ years experience creating and growing technology companies with customers in LATAM, US and Europe.
He has led Technology, Digital Marketing and Sales teams across different startups and worked as IT Consultant and Project Manager for companies like Microsoft and GE Energy.
He graduated from the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology (EPFL) in Lausanne Switzerland with a Master's degree in Management of Technology and Entrepreneurship and holds a BSc in Information Technologies from Tecnológico de Monterrey (ITESM) in Mexico.

Sergio has strong experience defining and executing digital strategies for Enterprise and Startup companies and is specialized in the intersection between Product and GTM. He is interested in the implementation of digital growth strategies across industries.

Areas of expertise include:
Venture Capital, Cross-functional leadership, Product Management, B2B Go-to-Market, CRM, Digital Strategy, Growth Hacking, Technology Commercialization, Enterprise Sales.

speakers.name: Ricardo Celaya
speakers.bio:
Ricardo Celaya es emprendedor e inversionista mexicano enfocado en la intersección entre tecnología, creatividad e innovación. Es MBA por la Universidad de Oxford e ingeniero industrial por el Tecnológico de Monterrey, formación que combina con una trayectoria activa en iniciativas de emprendimiento, inversión y estrategia empresarial en América y Europa. En 2018, Forbes México lo reconoció como uno de los 30 Promesas de los Negocios.

Actualmente es Managing Partner de Kolab Ventures, firma desde la que opera un fondo de venture capital de etapa temprana y el Future Founders Program, un programa de aceleración para la próxima generación de fundadores. Es también partner en Break Off Capital, donde impulsa proyectos de inversión en tecnologías emergentes.

Anteriormente cofundó y presidió ARTO Group, emprendimiento que consolidó su perfil como constructor de negocios con visión de largo plazo.

//

Ricardo Celaya is a Mexican entrepreneur and investor focused on the intersection of technology, creativity, and innovation. He holds an MBA from Oxford University and a degree in Industrial Engineering from Tecnológico de Monterrey, a background he combines with an active career in entrepreneurship, investment, and business strategy initiatives in the Americas and Europe. In 2018, Forbes Mexico recognized him as one of the 30 Business Promises.

He is currently the Managing Partner of Kolab Ventures, a firm that operates an early-stage venture capital fund and the Future Founders Program, an acceleration program for the next generation of founders. He is also a partner at Break Off Capital, where he promotes investment projects in emerging technologies.

Previously, he co-founded and chaired ARTO Group, a venture that solidified his profile as a business builder with a long-term vision.

speakers.name: Fernando Vera
speakers.bio:
Fernando Vera es un emprendedor que se ha desarrollado en áreas de ciberseguridad y el uso blockchain en diferentes industrias trabajando para empresas en Israel, San Francisco y diferentes partes de Latinoamérica.
Además, es delegado honorario de México en la ONU - New York por la organización Blockchain for Impact.
Actualmente es Cofundador y Director General de CINCEL (Plataforma líder de firma digital en México)

//

Fernando Vera is an entrepreneur who has developed expertise in cybersecurity and blockchain across various industries, working for companies in Israel, San Francisco, and different parts of Latin America.

He is also an honorary delegate of Mexico to the UN in New York for the organization Blockchain for Impact.
He is currently the Co-founder and CEO of CINCEL (a leading digital signature platform in Mexico).

speakers.name: Josue Delgado
speakers.bio:
Josué Delgado es un líder en innovación y emprendimiento en México, impulsando desde incMty iniciativas que conectan emprendedores, startups y ecosistemas para transformar ideas en proyectos de alto impacto. Se desempeña como Corporate Innovation Director en incMty, donde enfoca sus esfuerzos en innovación abierta, desarrollo corporativo y colaboración entre corporativos, academia y scaleups. Egresado del Tecnológico de Monterrey, es reconocido por su experiencia en venture capital y activación de ecosistemas de innovación

//

Josué Delgado is a Mexican innovation and entrepreneurship leader, driving initiatives at incMty that connect entrepreneurs, startups, and ecosystems to turn ideas into high‑impact projects. He serves as Corporate Innovation Director at incMty, focusing on open innovation, corporate development, and collaboration between corporations, academia, and scaleups. A graduate from Tecnológico de Monterrey, he is recognized for his expertise in venture capital and innovation ecosystem activation

events.name: Why is it important for universities to foster entrepreneurship?
events.description: The conference "Why is it important for universities to foster entrepreneurship?" addresses the key role of higher education institutions in training students capable of creating innovative solutions and generating value for society. Throughout the talk, it analyzes how universities can promote an entrepreneurial mindset, strengthen skills such as leadership and problem-solving, and facilitate the connection between academic knowledge, applied research, and market needs. In addition, it highlights strategies, programs, and examples, such as the Monterrey Innovation District, which demonstrate how entrepreneurship and innovation contribute to economic development, job creation, urban transformation, and social innovation.

speakers.name: Mario Adrian Flores
speakers.bio:
El Dr. Mario Adrián Flores ha formado parte del Tec de Monterrey desde hace 26 años, y se ha desempeñado como Director de División, Director de Departamento, Director de Campus Guadalajara, León e Irapuato y Vicepresidente de Región Occidente. Actualmente es vicepresidente de campus Monterrey del Tec de Monterrey, encargado de dirigir el plantel de estudios más grande de la institución, con más de 20 mil alumnos cada semestre; adicionalmente apoya en proyectos como Distrito de Innovación Monterrey, que busca impulsar la investigación aplicada y tecnología para las nuevas generaciones.

//

Dr. Mario Adrián Flores has been part of Tec de Monterrey for 26 years, and has served as Division Director, Department Director, Director of the Guadalajara, León and Irapuato Campuses and Vice President of the Western Region. He is currently vice president of Tec de Monterrey's Monterrey campus, in charge of directing the institution's largest campus, with more than 20 thousand students each semester; additionally, Mario Adrián supports projects such as the Monterrey Innovation District, which seeks to promote applied research and technology for new generations.

speakers.name: Angeles Vela
speakers.bio:
María de los Ángeles Vela del Río es Ingeniera Industrial y de Sistemas por la Universidad Iberoamericana, con Maestría en Administración por el Tecnológico de Monterrey. Su trayectoria incluye experiencia en el sector privado (calidad y producción) y consultoría para PyMEs. Colaboró en Desarrollo Económico del gobierno de Coahuila, enfocándose en PyMEs, logrando fondos y formando el clúster de plata y la red de Tecnologías de la Información en la región.

Actualmente, es la Directora General del Clúster TIC de Nuevo León, CSOFTMTY. Desde allí impulsa iniciativas de Emprendimiento e Innovación, Capital Humano y Mercados del ecosistema de TI. Ha promovido activamente el aumento de mujeres en la industria de TI y ha estimulado la capacitación en tecnología a más de 4,000 niños y jóvenes para carreras STEM.

Participa como Consejera y Comisionada en diversos organismos como Consejo Nuevo León, Venture Café Monterrey, Secretaría de Desarrollo Económico del Gobierno de Monterrey e IncMty. Es Tesorera de MXTI (Red de clústers de TI en México). Entre los logros del clúster bajo su dirección se encuentran la certificación ESCA ORO a nivel mundial, la formación de un Centro de Ciberseguridad y convenios con la Cámara del Pacífico y la Universidad de Santa Clara California. Su pasión es usar la tecnología para lograr un México más equitativo y feliz.

//

María de los Ángeles Vela del Río is an Industrial and Systems Engineer from the Universidad Iberoamericana, with a Master's degree in Administration from Tecnológico de Monterrey. Her career includes experience in the private sector (quality and production) and consulting for SMEs. She collaborated in Economic Development for the Coahuila government, focusing on SMEs, securing funding, and forming the silver cluster and the Information Technology network in the region.

Currently, she is the General Director of the Nuevo León IT Cluster, CSOFTMTY. From this role, she drives initiatives in Entrepreneurship and Innovation, Human Capital, and Markets within the IT ecosystem. She has actively promoted increasing the number of women in the IT industry and has stimulated technology training for over 4,000 children and young people for STEM careers.

She participates as a Counselor and Commissioner in various organizations such as Consejo Nuevo León, Venture Café Monterrey, the Secretariat of Economic Development of the Government of Monterrey, and IncMty. She is the Treasurer of MXTI (Network of IT clusters in Mexico). Among the cluster's achievements under her leadership are the ESCA GOLD certification worldwide, the formation of a Cybersecurity Center, and agreements with the Pacific Chamber and the University of Santa Clara California. Her passion is to use technology to achieve a more equitable and happy Mexico.

speakers.name: Sabrina Ortiz Martinez
speakers.position: Directora de Fomento a la Mujer
speakers.company: Secretaría de Economía Del Estado de Nuevo León
speakers.bio:
Sabrina Ortiz es Licenciada en Publicidad y Comunicación de Mercados por el Tecnológico de Monterrey y actualmente cursa la Maestría en Ciencias Políticas y Gobierno en la Universidad Autónoma de Nuevo León. Se desempeña como Directora de Fomento a la Mujer en la Secretaría de Economía de Nuevo León, donde diseña e impulsa estrategias para fortalecer la autonomía económica de las mujeres a través del emprendimiento, la formalización y el acceso a financiamiento y redes de apoyo.

Previamente colaboró en la Secretaría de las Mujeres del Estado de Nuevo León, desarrollando programas de acompañamiento y fortalecimiento para mujeres en situación de vulnerabilidad. Es también emprendedora de Amorfo, un proyecto de reciclaje de vidrio que integra innovación y sostenibilidad.

Su trabajo se centra en la convicción de que la independencia financiera no es una tendencia, sino una estrategia de justicia social y liderazgo femenino capaz de transformar generaciones.

//

Sabrina Ortiz holds a Bachelor’s degree in Advertising and Marketing Communications from Tecnológico de Monterrey and is currently pursuing a Master’s degree in Political Science and Government at the Autonomous University of Nuevo León. She serves as Director for Women’s Economic Development at the Ministry of Economy of Nuevo León, where she designs and implements strategies to strengthen women’s economic autonomy through entrepreneurship, business formalization, access to financing, and support networks.

She previously worked at the Ministry for Women of Nuevo León, developing programs focused on support and empowerment for women in vulnerable situations. She is also the founder of Amorfo, a glass recycling project that integrates innovation and sustainability.

Her work is driven by the conviction that financial independence is not a trend, but a strategy for social justice and female leadership capable of transforming generations.
